metadata_information.title: Collateralized Borrowing : Insights from The World Bank Enterprise Surveys
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: Collateralized Borrowing : Insights from The World Bank Enterprise Surveys

document_description.abstract: Using the World Bank Enterprise Surveys, we investigate the prevalence and correlates of collateralized borrowing in 131 countries between 2005-2017. Overall, 77 percent of loans require collateral, and the median loan-to-collateral value is 60 percent. Small firms and loans from non-bank financial institutions are less often associated with collateral. When collateral is pledged, the type of collateral assets is strongly correlated with the loan-to-value ratio.

An objective of the series is to get the findings out quickly, even if the presentations are less than fully polished. The papers carry the names of the authors and should be cited accordingly.
